data.title: Chinese embassy calls on US to end semiconductor export controls
data.description:
The Chinese embassy in the U.S. has called on the Trump administration to end the semiconductor export controls, as it believes they are being abused.

“Since the China-U.S. economic and trade talks in Geneva, both sides have maintained communication over their respective concerns in the economic and trade fields on various bilateral and multilateral occasions at multiple levels,” the embassy said in a statement obtained by CNBC. “Recently, China has repeatedly raised concerns with the U.S. regarding its abuse of export control measures in the semiconductor sector and other related practices. China once again urges the U.S. to immediately correct its erroneous actions, cease discriminatory restrictions against China and jointly uphold the consensus reached at the high-level talks in Geneva.”

The embassy's comments come after President Trump posted on social media this morning that the Chinese have violated their trade agreement with the U.S. Trump did not specify how the Chinese violated the agreement or in what fashion.

Related tickers: Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO), AMD (NASDAQ:AMD)

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: ECB ramps up focus on banks' exposure to private markets - report
data.description:
The European Central Bank is intensifying its focus on lenders' private markets exposure on concern that the quick adoption of related asset classes poses substantial new risks, according to a media report.

The banking regulator has indicated that it's sending letters to executives at some banks urging them to be careful with their practices in financing private funds, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

They also said the ECB plans to carry out on-site investigations at several major European banks. One of the first such visits were made to Société Générale (OTCPK:SCGLF) (OTCPK:SCGLY).

Regulators across the globe have taken notice of the rapid growth in private credit, with investors pouring capital into funds that don't get the same kind of scrutiny as banks. Traditional lenders have been looking to capitalize on the sector by providing some forms of leverage to private credit-related funds and their managers, the article said.

The latest moves follow a review that started last year that sought details from top European banks on their exposure to private credit firms and their lending to funds operating in the sector. The review found that the banks weren't able to properly assess their full exposure to private credit funds, Bloomberg said.

Such reviews are likely to affect a fairly small number of banks, such as those with sophisticated corporate banking activities. In general, European banks have been less involved in private equity and debt than their U.S. rivals.

"Private markets bring both benefits and risks for euro area financial stability, although data scarcity and opaqueness hinder a comprehensive risk assessment," according to the ECB's May 2025 Financial Stability Review.

The aggregate exposure to private market in the euro area isn't alarming, as insurers and pension funds, which typically have high risk-bearing capacities, are the main investors in private markets.

"But potential risks may be concealed in concentrated exposures, opaque interlinkages and bank lending exposure to private market players," it said. "Additionally, private-market-backed firms, and especially PE-backed firms, seem to continue to rely on banks after receiving private market funding, pointing to common exposures between banks and private markets."

Other large European banks include: Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB), Banco Santander (SAN),, BNP Paribas (OTCQX:BNPQF) (OTCQX:BNPQY), Crédit Agricole (OTCPK:CRARF) (CERARY), and UBS Group (UBS).

data.title: Lionsgate Studios gains as Mnuchin boosts stake to 12.6%
data.description:
Lionsgate Studios stock (NYSE:LION) quickly moved higher by 2.7% Friday on a report that former Hollywood producer and Treasury secretary Steven Mnuchin boosted his stake in the company.

Mnuchin's Liberty Strategic Capital added 3.3M additional common shares, according to Variety, to raise Mnuchin's holdings to control 35.99M shares -- about a 12.6% stake in the recently split-off company, up from 9.5%.

Lionsgate Studios formally split from the former Lions Gate Entertainment earlier this month. It had been trading separately since it merged with a special-purpose acquisition company last year, with Lions Gate Entertainment holding more than 87% of shares.

Those shareholders received shares in Lionsgate Studios and Starz Entertainment (STRZ) once the split was formalized.

data.title: Enterprise Products slips as license requirement could lead to lower China exports
data.description:
Enterprise Products Partners (NYSE:EPD) -1.2% in Friday's trading after saying its ethane and butane exports could be affected by a U.S. Department of Commerce requirement that it apply for a license to export to China.

The U.S. has ordered several companies to stop shipping goods, including ethane and butane, to China without a license and revoked licenses already granted to certain suppliers, Reuters reported earlier this week.

Enterprise Products (NYSE:EPD) said in an 8-K filing that it is evaluating its procedures and internal controls and cannot determine whether it will be able to successfully obtain any required license for applicable transactions involving covered ethane and butane products.

The company said its marine export terminal on the Houston Ship Channel loaded 213K bbl/day of ethane, with ~85K bbl/day, or 37%, went to Chinese markets.

"We are not able to ascertain whether these potential restrictions on the partnership's activities relating to covered ethane and butane products will have a material adverse effect... on the partnership's financial position, results of operations and cash flows," the company said in the filing.

data.title: Merck antibody drug conjugate asset shows antitumor activity in phase 2
data.description:
Merck's (NYSE:MRK) experimental antibody drug conjugate zilovertamab vedotin, in combination with standard of care, demonstrated encouraging antitumor activity in the phase 2 portion of a study of diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) patients.

Those who received the potential first-in-class ADC at the 1.75 mg/kg dose in combination with rituximab and gemcitabine-oxaliplatin saw a 56.3% objective response rate (ORR), with eight complete responses  and one partial response.

At a median follow-up of 18.1 months, those who received the 1.5 mg/kg dose of zilovertamab vedotin had an ORR of 26.7% and median duration of response (DOR) was 14.4 months. At a median follow-up of 9.9 months, those on the 1.75 mg/kg dose saw an ORR of 56.3% and median DOR of 8.7 months. At a median follow-up of 9.3 months, patients on the 2.0 mg/kg dose saw an ORR of 57.1% while median DOR was not reached.

Zilovertamab vedotin is being evaluated in patients with previously untreated DLBCL in the phase 3 waveLINE-010 study and in the phase 2 waveLINE-007 study. The newest data is from the phase 2 dose confirmation portion of the phase 2/3 waveLINE-003 study.

Zilovertamab vedotin targets receptor tyrosine kinase-like orphan receptor 1 (ROR1).

data.title: PrairieSky renews share buyback program for up to 15.4M shares
data.description:
• PrairieSky Royalty (OTCPK:PREKF) received TSX approval to renew its normal course issuer bid (NCIB).
  • The renewed NCIB allows for the repurchase of up to 15.36M common shares (6.5% of shares outstanding).
  • The program begins June 4, 2025, and ends no later than June 3, 2026. All repurchased shares will be cancelled.
  • PrairieSky believes its stock may trade below intrinsic value, making buybacks beneficial to shareholders.
  • Since 2016, the company has cancelled 20.1M shares at an average price of $16.74.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: EssilorLuxottica to acquire Optegra in med-tech expansion
data.description:
• EssilorLuxottica (OTCPK:ESLOY) has agreed to acquire Optegra, a leading ophthalmology group operating in the UK, Czech Republic, Poland, Slovakia, and the Netherlands.
  • The move strengthens EssilorLuxottica’s med-tech strategy, expanding its reach beyond eyewear into advanced eyecare and surgical solutions.
  • Optegra operates 70+ eye hospitals and diagnostic centers under the Optegra, Lexum, and Iris brands.
  • Services include cataract, glaucoma, and AMD treatments, as well as refractive lens and laser eye surgeries.
  • The platform integrates AI in diagnostics and surgical planning, serving both public and private patients.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Galaxy Digital prices upsized stock offering; shares down
data.description:
• Galaxy Digital (NASDAQ:GLXY) priced an upsized underwritten public offering of 31.6M Class A common shares at $19 each.
  • The offering includes 26.4M shares from Galaxy and 5.2M from existing shareholders.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to 4.74M additional secondary shares.
  • Net proceeds will be used to purchase LP Units from Galaxy Digital Holdings LP. Funds will support AI and high-performance computing expansion at its Helios data center in West Texas.
  • Galaxy will not receive proceeds from shares sold by existing shareholders.
  • The offering is expected to close on June 3, 2025.
  • Shares down 6.3% on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Regeneron slumps as Wells Fargo cuts on COPD trial data
data.description:
Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) shares fell ~18% to reach a new 52-week low as a Phase 3 trial failure for its antibody therapy itepekimab, developed with Sanofi (SNY) for a lung disorder known as chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), prompted Wells Fargo to downgrade the stock.

Itepekimab, targeted at COPD-linked inflammatory agent interleukin-33, failed to reach the primary endpoint in a late-stage trial in their AERIFY clinical program, the companies said in a press release on Friday.

While the drug, a key component in Tarrytown, New York-based biotech’s pipeline, hit the main goal in another late-stage trial for the same indication, “We think itepekimab’s disappointing data create a big challenge for REGN in the long term,” Wells Fargo analyst Mohit Bansal wrote.

He argued that the itepekimab infusion represented a major driver for Regeneron’s (NASDAQ:REGN) prospects in 2031 when its asthma therapy developed with Sanofi (OTCPK:SNYNF) is expected to lose market exclusivity.

“We think investors are less excited about the rest of the pipeline,” the analyst added as he downgraded REGN to Equal Weight from Overweight, citing a lack of value-unlocking catalysts in the near term.  Bansal kept his $580 per share on the stock unchanged.

However, analysts from Cantor Fitzgerald, Oppenheimer, and JPMorgan were among those to reiterate their buy-equivalent ratings on Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) despite the trial setback.

“We do still see a role for the drug but expect the program will need a 2nd positive phase 3 trial for approval and anticipate today’s update likely represents a 2-3 year delay to market,” Bloomberg News reported, quoting JPMorgan’s Chris Schott as saying with an Overweight rating.

Meanwhile, Cantor’s Carter Gould, who has a similar rating on the stock, added, “We still recommend REGN shares given the valuation support, Dupixent’s runway, and low expectations ascribed to the pipeline, but we expect shares to be down meaningfully today.”

data.title: Pfizer meets main goals in colorectal cancer treatment
data.description:
Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) said on Friday that a late-stage trial evaluating a Braftovi combination increased survival time for patients with metastatic colorectal cancer with a BRAF V600E mutation.

In the study, Braftovi in combination with Erbitux and mFOLFOX6, showed 51% risk reduction in death compared to standard-of-care treatment.

Braftovi combination regimen also showed 47% risk reduction in disease progression or death compared to standard-of-care treatment, meeting the trial’s dual main goal of progression-free survival.

The safety profile was in line with existing data for the individual drugs, with no new safety concerns identified. Common side effects included nausea, anemia, diarrhea, decreased appetite, vomiting, low white blood cell count, joint pain, and rash.

About 13.8% of patients discontinued Braftovi due to side effects, the company said.
